name: Brickle Peanut Butter Cookies
recipeIngredient: 1 1/4 cup Firmly packed Light Brown Sugar
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F.
2. In a large bowl, combine brown sugar, peanut butter, shortening, milk, and vanilla extract. Beat at medium speed with an electric mixer until well blended.
3. Add egg, beating until just blended.
4. Combine flour, salt, and baking soda. Add to creamed mixture at low speed. Stir in Heath Crunch Bits or Toffee Chips. Mix until just combined.
5. Drop dough by heaping teaspoonfuls about 2 inches apart onto an ungreased baking sheet.
6. Flatten slightly with the back of a spoon or the bottom of a glass.
7. Bake on the baking sheet at a time, 375°F for 7-8 minutes, or until set and just beginning to brown. Do not overbake. Cool on the baking sheet for 2 minutes before removing cookies to foil or racks to cool completely.
Icing:
1. Combine confectioners' sugar, Crisco, and vanilla extract in a medium bowl. Beat at low speed.
2. Add milk as needed to make a good spreading consistency.
3. Spread icing over the center tops of cooled cookies.
4. Topping: Dip each cookie into Heath Crunch Bits or Toffee Chips and gently press them into the icing. Allow icing to set before serving.
